meta.title: Predictive Mission Action
meta.subtitle: Live command analysis from safe synthetic output objects

findings.title: Lead signal
findings.detail: Cross-source activity rose before link quality degraded.

findings.title: Signal shift
findings.detail: Activity and emission channels moved outside the recent envelope.

findings.title: Route exposure
findings.detail: Route B carries higher projected load and lower coverage.

findings.title: Forecast confidence
findings.detail: Near-horizon confidence remains above the action threshold.

actions.title: Adjust route
actions.detail: Move priority traffic to Route A and preserve Route B for contingency.

actions.title: Increase coverage
actions.detail: Reposition Relay 5 and widen the observation window.

actions.title: Stage reserve
actions.detail: Advance reserve capacity before the projected demand peak.

actions.title: Extend observation
actions.detail: Hold the current posture for two additional sampling windows.

advisory.response: Readiness remains serviceable, but three linked indicators changed in sequence: activity rose, link quality weakened, and Route B confidence declined. The near-horizon projection favors an early reversible adjustment rather than waiting for the decision window.

advisory.prompt: What changed before the lead signal?
advisory.response: Observed activity increased across two sources while corridor load was still within its recent range. The change preceded the link-quality decline by approximately eleven sampling windows, making it useful as an early indicator in this display.

Route A retains stronger coverage and a wider reserve margin. Route B is shorter but intersects the highest projected load band and has the weaker relay path. The display therefore ranks Route A first for priority movement.

advisory.prompt: What is the most important uncertainty?
advisory.response: The largest uncertainty is whether the activity surge will persist beyond the current forecast window. Confidence decays gradually after the cutoff, so the recommended actions are deliberately reversible and should be reassessed at the next evidence checkpoint.

First, shift priority traffic to Route A. Second, reposition Relay 5 to restore coverage depth. Third, stage reserve capacity before the projected demand peak. Recheck the plan after two sampling windows.
